result.summary: Hardened device-capability detection with the AGGRESSIVE strategy. usePerfTier now lifts the cores threshold from <4 to <6, adds a cached getGPUTier() probe (offscreen canvas + WEBGL_debug_renderer_info, regex-matches SwiftShader/llvmpipe/Microsoft Basic Render/Mesa/VirtualBox/software/ANGLE software), adds a fire-and-forget Battery API probe, flags Windows UA + cores<8 as low, and exports a new useDeviceCapabilities() hook returning {tier,isMobile,isTouch,gpuTier,prefersReducedMotion,isWindows,saveData,isLowEnd}. useIsMobile widens to viewport<=1024 plus the same low-end signals (cores<6, deviceMemory<4, reduced-motion, gpuTier low, save-data, coarse pointer). SceneContainer now consumes useDeviceCapabilities() and falls back to SvgCoreFallback when isMobile/tier=low/gpuTier=low; the dynamic <Scene/> is wrapped in a local WebGLErrorBoundary class component that swaps to the SVG fallback if context creation throws. All browser API access is SSR-safe; existing usePerfTier/readPerfTier/default exports are preserved. typecheck and next build both pass clean.
